title: anacamptis papilionacea subsp. heroica
intro: Anacamptis papilionacea subsp. heroica, also known as the green-winged orchid, is a rare subspecies of orchid native to the Iberian Peninsula and parts of southern Europe. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are believed to be due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The green-winged orchid is said to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving respiratory issues, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and leaves are typically used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use is not well-documented and more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
